frameworks.name: spatial computing
frameworks.one_line: item-specific activity flows spatially across the network during a WM task

frameworks.name: beta-gamma oscillation interactions
frameworks.one_line: burst interactions are proposed to drive the spatial flow of WM content

frameworks.name: low-dimensional population coding
frameworks.one_line: spatial flow appears as low-dimensional shared activity across many neurons

core_question: How can coarse beta and gamma oscillations selectively control individual items held in working memory?
key_assumptions: WM control reflects interactions between beta and gamma bursts
item-specific activity is supported by recurrent connectivity
control information can be carried by spatial layout independent of recurrence
manipulations: task structure that requires holding and ordering multiple WM items
key_findings: item-specific activity flows spatially across the network across the trial
spatial flow is reflected in low-dimensional population activity
local field potentials and spiking are consistent with the spatial-flow prediction
interpretations: control-related information (e.g. item order) is stored in spatial activity, not in recurrent weights
spatial computing may support generalization and zero-shot learning by adding a spatial encoding dimension
